story: Detective Elijah Crain chased the abductor of Doverton’s girls. Sgt. Dana Rowen, his odd partner, always sucked butterscotch and handed it out. Emma Darcy was found with a half‑dissolved butterscotch blocking her airway. Crain’s suspicion veered toward Rowen; night guard Martin Nelson’s gentle comments fanned it. When another girl washed up, Crain melted down at the scene and was jailed as a madman. Rowen kept working; Nelson became the humble face at vigils. After one more interview he went home, whistling, unlocked his basement—and Leah Mercer raised her head from the dark.
implausible_knowledge: and Leah Mercer raised her head from the dark
question: In the days that follow, as Rowen plans her next step on Leah Mercer’s case, what is she most likely to do?
answer_with_knowledge: Obtain a warrant and lead a search of Martin Nelson’s home, going straight to his basement to rescue Leah.
answer_without_knowledge: Recanvass Leah’s last known route and ask Martin Nelson to pass along any tips while she reviews case files.
